fort-st-john-plateau.name: Highway Camp
fort-st-john-plateau.description: The staging area where Highway 97 meets the access road. Last chance for civilization.

fort-st-john-plateau.name: Pipeline Crossing
fort-st-john-plateau.description: The road crosses over the main gas pipeline. Heavy truck traffic.

fort-st-john-plateau.name: Muskeg Flats
fort-st-john-plateau.description: Treacherous peatland that swallows machinery whole if you stray from the road.

fort-st-john-plateau.name: Doig River Camp
fort-st-john-plateau.description: Camp near Doig River First Nation territory. Community relations are key here.

fort-st-john-plateau.name: Spruce Ridge
fort-st-john-plateau.description: Climbing up through dense spruce forest. The grade gets steep.

fort-st-john-plateau.name: Plateau Summit
fort-st-john-plateau.description: Top of the plateau. Wind howls across the exposed landscape.

fort-st-john-plateau.name: Block A-7 Landing
fort-st-john-plateau.description: Active cutblock with equipment staging. Resupply possible from other crews.

fort-st-john-plateau.name: Wetland Bypass
fort-st-john-plateau.description: A long detour around sensitive wetland habitat.

fort-st-john-plateau.name: Gas Well Access
fort-st-john-plateau.description: Industrial access road serving several gas wells. Watch for tanker trucks.

fort-st-john-plateau.name: North Muskeg
fort-st-john-plateau.description: More peatland. Someone got a Cat stuck here last spring.

fort-st-john-plateau.name: Caribou Flats
fort-st-john-plateau.description: Open area frequented by caribou herds. Tread carefully during migration.

fort-st-john-plateau.name: Final Destination - Block A-12
fort-st-john-plateau.description: You've reached the target block. Time to set up base camp.

muskwa-foothills.name: Fort Nelson Staging
muskwa-foothills.description: The last town before wilderness. Stock up while you can.

muskwa-foothills.name: Alaska Highway Turnoff
muskwa-foothills.description: Leaving the main highway onto a forest service road.

muskwa-foothills.name: Prophet River
muskwa-foothills.description: Major river crossing. Check water levels before attempting.

muskwa-foothills.name: Foothills Camp
muskwa-foothills.description: Entering the foothills. Terrain gets rougher from here.

muskwa-foothills.name: Permafrost Zone
muskwa-foothills.description: Ground shifts and heaves. The road surface is unpredictable.

muskwa-foothills.name: Caribou Corridor
muskwa-foothills.description: Critical caribou migration route. Operations may be restricted.

muskwa-foothills.name: Mountain Pass
muskwa-foothills.description: Steep climb through a narrow pass. No room for error.

muskwa-foothills.name: Alpine Meadow
muskwa-foothills.description: Brief respite in a high alpine meadow. Beautiful but exposed.

muskwa-foothills.name: Remote Camp Alpha
muskwa-foothills.description: Helicopter-supplied remote camp. Limited resupply possible.

muskwa-foothills.name: Grizzly Creek
muskwa-foothills.description: Named for obvious reasons. Stay alert.

muskwa-foothills.name: Final Ridge
muskwa-foothills.description: Last climb before the destination. Crew is getting tired.

muskwa-foothills.name: Muskwa Summit Block
muskwa-foothills.description: You've made it to the most remote block in the district.

bulkley-valley.name: Smithers Yard
bulkley-valley.description: Mill yard on the edge of Smithers. Hudson Bay Mountain looms above town like a postcard.

bulkley-valley.name: Telkwa River Bridge
bulkley-valley.description: Single-lane log bridge over the Telkwa. The timbers creak under a loaded truck.

bulkley-valley.name: Ranch Gate
bulkley-valley.description: The FSR cuts through open ranch land. Close the gate behind you or hear about it later.

bulkley-valley.name: Wet'suwet'en Territory Marker
bulkley-valley.description: Entering Cas Yikh house territory. Protocol requires notification before operations begin.

bulkley-valley.name: Birch Bench Camp
bulkley-valley.description: Crew camp tucked into a stand of paper birch. Good water from the creek below.

bulkley-valley.name: VQO Ridgeline
bulkley-valley.description: This ridge is visible from Highway 16. Every stump placement matters for the visual quality objective.

bulkley-valley.name: Water Intake Buffer
bulkley-valley.description: Community water intake 200 m downslope. Sediment control is not optional here.

bulkley-valley.name: Spruce-Birch Mixedwood
bulkley-valley.description: Classic SBSmc2 stand. Hybrid spruce and birch fighting for canopy position in every gap.

bulkley-valley.name: Decker Lake Road Junction
bulkley-valley.description: FSR junction with a fuel cache and radio repeater. Contractor trucks resupply here.

bulkley-valley.name: Morice River Overlook
bulkley-valley.description: The road climbs above the Morice valley. Coho spawning channels glint silver far below.

bulkley-valley.name: Foggy Bench
bulkley-valley.description: Valley fog pools here most mornings. Visibility under 50 m until it burns off by ten.

bulkley-valley.name: Block BV-12 - Escarpment Edge
bulkley-valley.description: Destination block perched on the Bulkley escarpment. The view is worth the drive.

fraser-plateau.name: Hixon Staging
fraser-plateau.description: Gravel lot south of Prince George where the crew musters. Coffee from the gas station across the road.

fraser-plateau.name: Blackwater Road
fraser-plateau.description: Long straight FSR through beetle-killed pine. Grey snags line both sides like a dead army.

fraser-plateau.name: Old Burn Edge
fraser-plateau.description: 2017 wildfire scar starts here. Fireweed and lodgepole regen carpet the charred ground.

fraser-plateau.name: Lheidli T'enneh Camp
fraser-plateau.description: Camp at the edge of Lheidli T'enneh traditional territory. Collaborative monitoring underway.

fraser-plateau.name: Pine Plantation Corridor
fraser-plateau.description: Rows of 15-year-old planted pine from the first salvage wave. Spacing audit country.

fraser-plateau.name: Dry Creek Gully
fraser-plateau.description: A seasonal creek bed that turns into a washout after rain. Culvert is undersized.

fraser-plateau.name: Plateau Overlook
fraser-plateau.description: Open SBSwk1 bench with views across fifty kilometres of rolling green and grey. Wind never stops.

fraser-plateau.name: Wildfire Guard Station
fraser-plateau.description: BC Wildfire Service forward cache. Fuel, water, and a sat phone if things go sideways.

fraser-plateau.name: Aspen Blowdown
fraser-plateau.description: A winter windstorm flattened this aspen stand. Chainsaw work just to get through.

fraser-plateau.name: Nazko Road Junction
fraser-plateau.description: Where the main FSR meets the Nazko road. Occasional ranch traffic and dust clouds.

fraser-plateau.name: Block FP-11 - Resilience Trial
fraser-plateau.description: Destination block in a wildfire resilience trial area. Mixed-species planting and fuel breaks.

skeena-nass.name: Terrace Log Sort
skeena-nass.description: Sorting yard on the Skeena floodplain. Rain drips off everything. Always.

skeena-nass.name: Skeena River Crossing
skeena-nass.description: Steel bridge over the grey-green Skeena. Spring freshet can close this for days.

skeena-nass.name: Hemlock Canyon
skeena-nass.description: Deep-cut canyon draped in old-growth western hemlock. Moss hangs a metre thick from every branch.

skeena-nass.name: Gitanyow Checkpoint
skeena-nass.description: Gitanyow forest stewardship checkpoint. Permits verified and cultural sites confirmed.

skeena-nass.name: Karst Sinkhole Bench
skeena-nass.description: Limestone karst topography with sinkholes and disappearing streams. Watch your step off the road.

skeena-nass.name: Salmon Creek Crossing
skeena-nass.description: Class A fish stream with coho and steelhead. In-stream work window is July 15 to August 31. Period.

skeena-nass.name: Redcedar Bottom
skeena-nass.description: Valley-bottom redcedar stand so thick sunlight barely reaches the ground. Cathedral-quiet.

skeena-nass.name: Nass Camp
skeena-nass.description: Nisga'a Lisims government liaison camp at the head of the Nass valley. Resupply by truck weekly.

skeena-nass.name: Fog Ridge
skeena-nass.description: Coastal fog banks roll in from the west and stall on this ridge. Visibility can drop to zero.

skeena-nass.name: Spruce-Hemlock Bluffs
skeena-nass.description: Sitka spruce and hemlock on exposed bluffs above the river. Wind-shaped crowns lean inland.

skeena-nass.name: Lava Bed Flats
skeena-nass.description: Nisga'a Memorial Lava Bed edges the road. Volcanic rock and thin soils make for rough going.

skeena-nass.name: Block SN-12 - Nass Headwaters
skeena-nass.description: Destination block in the upper Nass. Rain-soaked CWH at its finest. Bring an extra dry bag.

tahltan-highland.name: Dease Lake Compound
tahltan-highland.description: Government compound at Dease Lake. The fuel truck comes twice a month if the road holds.

tahltan-highland.name: Telegraph Creek Road
tahltan-highland.description: Notorious single-lane road carved into canyon walls. Switchbacks with 500 m drops.

tahltan-highland.name: Stikine River Crossing
tahltan-highland.description: Reaction ferry across the Stikine. Current runs fast with glacial melt. Ferry shuts at dusk.

tahltan-highland.name: Tahltan Village Camp
tahltan-highland.description: Camp near Tahltan Nation village. Elders consult on cedar harvest boundaries each season.

tahltan-highland.name: Glacial Moraine
tahltan-highland.description: Unstable glacial till deposited by a retreating glacier. Boulders shift without warning.

tahltan-highland.name: Subalpine Parkland
tahltan-highland.description: Sparse Engelmann spruce and subalpine fir in wind-blasted parkland. Tree line is close.

tahltan-highland.name: Ealue Lake Cache
tahltan-highland.description: Fly-in supply cache on Ealue Lake. Bush plane drops gear when the ceiling lifts.

tahltan-highland.name: Willow Scrub Plateau
tahltan-highland.description: Dense willow and dwarf birch across a windswept plateau. Grizzly country through and through.

tahltan-highland.name: Outburst Channel
tahltan-highland.description: Glacial lake outburst flood channel. The last event stripped the road to bedrock.

tahltan-highland.name: Wolverine Pass
tahltan-highland.description: High pass above tree line. Snow possible any month. Named after the residents, not the comic book.

tahltan-highland.name: Block TH-11 - Highland Bench
tahltan-highland.description: Destination block on a subarctic bench. The most remote timber in the province, bar none.
